video_title: Happy Gilmore 2 | Official Teaser Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: I’m gonna make a prediction for this movie. Almost 30 years after the original, they will all have forgotten completely how to play these characters. Happy was sort of a straight laced character, didn’t talk in a funny voice. Well I bet my life Adam Sandler goofballs him up really badly in this one. Also, shooter. McGavin was kind of a straight laced, bad guy in the first one, but I am very confident that he’s going to overdo it in this one and make shooter, a Disney villain, or a goofball villain. Shooters comedy in the first one came from the script and the delivery. It was about the well written script that made it funny. I bet in this one, it’s gonna be very forced and the actor who plays shooter is going to basically be doing a caricature of his character from the first one. I hope I’m wrong, but that’s typically the way these years later sequels go, they forget what made the original great and they overdo the goofiness

comment_text: I totally understand that Netflix paid a shitload of money to be Adam's new home, but it would have been really great for this to be a box office exclusive for awhile; make it feel special, bring comedies back to the movie theater.  Hopefully they didn't screw this up. I really hope they paid huge for a good editor, bc the number 1 problem I see with sequels like this and Dumb and Dumber to (other than having a really poor script and having no real reason to exist) is poor editing.  Please don't have us spend a ton of downtime with these characters all just being goofy together, keep the plot moving along and have scenes flow; just scene, to scene, good editing, good music. Do not just give us a bunch of quiet goofiness while sitting with these characters having nothing to say for too long.

comment_text: Fun facts: Adam Sandler signed a multi million dollar contract with Netflix to make 6 movies exclusively for their platform.
